message.content:
Analyze the package.json dependencies and the recent upgrade:

1. Read packages/tools/package.json to see current dependencies
2. Check if there are any peer dependency warnings or conflicts
3. Look at the pnpm-lock.yaml for any resolution issues
4. Identify any deprecated packages or breaking changes that might affect the code
5. Check tsconfig.json and biome.json for any configuration issues

Report any potential compatibility issues after the upgrade.

content.text: I'll analyze the package.json dependencies and check for any compatibility issues after the recent upgrade. Let me start by examining the project structure and key configuration files.

content.content: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read

toolUseResult: Error: EISDIR: illegal operation on a directory, read

content.content:
1→{
     2→  "name": "belt",
     3→  "version": "0.1.0",
     4→  "private": true,
     5→  "description": "TypeScript utility library with runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers",
     6→  "repository": {
     7→    "type": "git",
     8→    "url": "https://github.com/antoniopresto/belt"
     9→  },
    10→  "author": "@antoniopresto <antoniopresto@gmail.com>",
    11→  "packageManager": "pnpm@9.14.2",
    12→  "engines": {
    13→    "node": ">=20.0.0"
    14→  },
    15→  "scripts": {
    16→    "build": "turbo run build",
    17→    "dev": "turbo run dev",
    18→    "lint": "biome check .",
    19→    "lint:fix": "biome check --write .",
    20→    "format": "biome format --write .",
    21→    "test": "turbo run test",
    22→    "typecheck": "turbo run typecheck",
    23→    "clean": "turbo run clean && rm -rf node_modules",
    24→    "prepare": "husky"
    25→  },
    26→  "devDependencies": {
    27→    "@biomejs/biome": "^2.3.11",
    28→    "@types/node": "^25.0.3",
    29→    "husky": "9.1.7",
    30→    "turbo": "^2.7.2",
    31→    "typescript": "^5.9.3"
    32→  }
    33→}
    34→

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>
